public_evidence: dbGaP identifies this as the NIDDK International IBD Genetics Consortium Repository Global Screening Array. Public metadata describe a multicenter GWAS meta-analysis/resource using data from IIBDGC members and collaborators, with data from over 30 cohorts and 10,503 consented subjects in the current dbGaP version. Individual-level genotypes are available when permitted, while some groups can provide only summary statistics because of institutional or governmental restrictions.
recommended_policy: Controlled-access genetics resources that aggregate multiple cohorts, meta-analysis inputs, or repository submissions should remain discoverable as genetics resources but should not be counted as independent cohorts unless public metadata defines a single non-overlapping cohort. Count DS035 once only in a resource/dataset inventory, not in de-duplicated cohort counts.
recommended_overlap_notes: Controlled-access IIBDGC/NIDDK Global Screening Array GWAS resource. Public dbGaP/EGA metadata describe a multicenter GWAS meta-analysis/resource drawing on data from over 30 cohorts, with 10,503 consented subjects in the current dbGaP version and individual-level genotype data available only where permitted. This is not a clean single independent cohort row; keep it discoverable as one controlled-access genetics resource, but exclude from de-duplicated cohort counts. Do not split or sum component cohorts from public metadata alone.
uncertainty_notes: Public metadata do not expose a component cohort manifest or subject-level overlap map. The 10,503 figure is valid as dbGaP's consented-subject count for this version, not as a single-cohort count. Some contributing groups may provide only summary statistics, so the row is not uniformly individual-level across all source cohorts.
